title: Stelis rostratissima
taxonomy: Stelis rostratissima is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid that belongs to the genus Stelis, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. The genus Stelis is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is a part of the tribe Epidendreae, which is a significant group within the orchid family. This classification places Stelis rostratissima within the larger context of orchid systematics.

intro: Stelis rostratissima, also known as Antelope Acacia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Stelis rostratissima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
